questions.scenario: A deployment succeeds in `devpolaris-orders-dev` but fails in `devpolaris-orders-prod` because Cloud Run is not enabled there. What does this prove?

options.text: API enablement is project-specific, so success in one project does not prove another project is ready.
Cloud Run can never run in production projects.
The container image must be missing from every project.
Billing accounts are the same thing as service APIs.

questions.explanation: Many services must be enabled per project. Project context is part of the deployment target.

questions.scenario: An engineer searches for a Cloud SQL instance while their CLI and console are pointed at the staging project. They cannot find production. What should they check first?

options.text: The selected project ID, because the production resource may live in a different project.
The bucket object prefix, because prefixes select GCP projects.
The Cloud Run revision suffix, because it controls the console project picker.
The folder display color, because colors decide where resources exist.

questions.explanation: In GCP, project selection is everyday context. Looking in the wrong project can make real resources look missing.

questions.scenario: Which facts belong in a first project context note for production?

options.text: Project ID and, when useful, project number.
Enabled APIs needed by the service.
Runtime service account and deployer identity.
Only the friendly display name, because that is enough for automation.

questions.explanation: A useful project note contains strong context. Display names are human-friendly, but automation and review need stronger identifiers.
